name: Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet
region_intro: Jogging around Olèrdola offers a blend of natural landscapes and historical sites, situated between the Penedès plain and the Garraf foothills. The region features a Mediterranean environment with cultivation fields, shrubland, and thickets, including native dwarf palms. Trails traverse holm oak and cork oak groves, as well as pine forests, providing varied terrain for running. The area also incorporates historical vestiges like the Olèrdola Monumental Complex, allowing for runs amidst ancient walls and medieval structures. Best jogging routes around Olèrdola The most popular jogging route is Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views, including the sea and Montserrat mountain. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route explores the historical Olèrdola Monumental Complex, passing by the Sant Miquel church and ancient ruins. Local runners also love the View of Castell d'Olèrdola loop from Moja, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through varied Mediterranean landscapes, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Olèrdola is defined by Mediterranean flora, historical landmarks, and scenic vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easily accessible paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Olèrdola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Olèrdola?
A: Olèrdola offers a selection of 16 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include 1 easy route, 8 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual joggers and experienced trail runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Olèrdola?
A: The running trails in Olèrdola feature a diverse Mediterranean landscape. You'll encounter cultivation fields, shrubland, and thickets, including unique dwarf palm groves. Routes often traverse holm oak and cork oak groves, as well as pine forests, offering varied terrain from easily accessible paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging running routes in Olèrdola for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Olèrdola provides several challenging options. There are 7 difficult running routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131419944/puig-del-aguila-circular-por-la-sierra-de-cogullada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.5 km trail with notable elevation changes, offering expansive views.

Q: Can I find easy running routes suitable for beginners in Olèrdola?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy running route in Olèrdola, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog. The region's network includes paths that are less demanding, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty at their own pace.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Olèrdola?
A: Olèrdola is rich in history. Many routes pass by or through the &lt;strong&gt;Olèrdola Monumental Complex&lt;/strong&gt;, where you can run amidst ancient walls, the 12th-century Sant Miquel church, a Roman cistern, and remains of an Iberian settlement and medieval castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118873948/castillo-de-sant-miquel-d-olerdola-circular-por-las-rocas-de-can-ximet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores this historical area.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the running trails in Olèrdola?
A: Absolutely. From various points, such as the Pico del Águila (Eagle's Peak), runners can enjoy expansive views that encompass the sea, Montserrat mountain, and even the Pyrenees on clear days. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131419944/puig-del-aguila-circular-por-la-sierra-de-cogullada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its stunning vistas.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Olèrdola?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Olèrdola are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131419944/puig-del-aguila-circular-por-la-sierra-de-cogullada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118873948/castillo-de-sant-miquel-d-olerdola-circular-por-las-rocas-de-can-ximet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient loops through the region's landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Olèrdola?
A: Olèrdola's mild Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for running year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any natural features or attractions to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the diverse flora like holm oaks, cork oaks, and unique dwarf palms, you can also find natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; summit, offering panoramic views. The region's landscape is characterized by Aleppo pine forests and dry pastures, providing a typical Mediterranean backdrop for your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Olèrdola?
A: Runners frequently praise the unique combination of natural beauty and historical discovery that Olèrdola offers. The opportunity to jog through varied Mediterranean landscapes while encountering ancient ruins and medieval structures is a primary draw, making each run an enriching experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: While the immediate jogging routes may not directly border large lakes, the broader area around Olèrdola features natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes might offer distant views or pass through areas influenced by such water bodies.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the 1 easy route available in Olèrdola would be the most suitable starting point for families or those with younger children. These paths generally have less elevation gain and are easier to navigate.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Olèrdola?
A: The Parc d'Olèrdola, which hosts many of the running trails, is generally welcoming to dogs, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Specific rules may vary, so check local signage or park regulations for the most current information.
tips: This is the path that will take us to the Castillo de Sant Miquel and that gives us good views of the plain of Vilafranca del Penedès.

The Monumental Complex of Olèrdola includes the castle of Olèrdola along with other ruins, such as the Roman wall or the silos or cisterns, and also the church of San Miguel de Olèrdola (10th century). In the same area is the Museum of Archeology of Catalonia-Olèrdola, which can be visited from Tuesday to Sunday.
An abric is a balma-shaped rock formation that has the shape of a cliff and is named after the nearby farm/house. The path that crosses it is beautiful with good views of the valley below.
